# cli/reference/1.0.0 API

> Full LLM content for cli/reference/1.0.0

This file contains all documentation content in a single document following the llmstxt.org standard.

## 1.0.0

# CLI Reference

Agent customization portability for AI coding tools

## convert

Convert agent customization between tools

### Arguments

- `[path]` - Project path (Default: `.`)

### Options

- `-f, --from <tool>` **(required)** - Source tool (available: cursor, claude, a16n, agentsmd)
- `-t, --to <tool>` **(required)** - Target tool (available: cursor, claude, a16n, agentsmd)
- `--dry-run` - Show what would happen without writing
- `--json` - Output as JSON
- `-q, --quiet` - Suppress non-error output
- `-v, --verbose` - Show detailed output
- `--gitignore-output-with <style>` - Manage git-ignore status of output files (Default: `none`)
- `--if-gitignore-conflict <resolution>` - How to resolve git-ignore conflicts in match mode (Default: `skip`)
- `--delete-source` - Delete source files after successful conversion (skipped sources are preserved)
- `--from-dir <dir>` - Override source directory for reading (discover). Default: positional [path]
- `--to-dir <dir>` - Override target directory for writing (emit). Default: positional [path]
- `--rewrite-path-refs` - Rewrite file path references in content to point to target-format paths

## discover

List agent customization without converting

### Arguments

- `[path]` - Project path (Default: `.`)

### Options

- `-f, --from <tool>` **(required)** - Tool to discover (available: cursor, claude, a16n, agentsmd)
- `--json` - Output as JSON
- `-v, --verbose` - Show detailed output
- `--from-dir <dir>` - Override source directory for reading. Default: positional [path]

## plugins

Show available plugins
